mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS has positive 3-month growth, stronger household spending raises expected corporate revenues and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days; negative RSAFS momentum implies the opposite signal.
failure_condition: Reject if the RSAFS 3-month growth signal has Rank IC below 0.03 over two consecutive 3-year rolling windows or positive RSAFS momentum does not deliver higher 20-day returns than negative momentum.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS crosses above its 12-month moving average after rising at least 25 basis points in 3 months, higher discount rates reduce equity duration value and predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above its 12-month moving average after a >= 0.25pp 3-month increase
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if triggered mean returns are not below unconditional mean returns at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in UNRATE and slowdown in PAYEMS growth signals deteriorating labor income and recession risk, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months and PAYEMS 3-month growth falls below its 12-month average
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor trigger has nonnegative average 60-day forward returns or if either proxy alone explains the full signal with no incremental interaction term at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO and RSAFS both post positive 3-month growth, synchronized production and consumption expansion raises earnings expectations and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint positive-growth state does not outperform states with only one positive series, or if the interaction coefficient is not positive in walk-forward regressions.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST crosses below its 12-month moving average with a year-over-year decline greater than 10%, weakening residential investment reduces cyclical earnings expectations and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below its 12-month moving average and YoY growth < -10%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered housing weakness is followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of instances or if drawdown frequency is not above the unconditional baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation acceleration occurs while FEDFUNDS is already elevated, markets price tighter policy and lower real earnings multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth exceeds its 6-month annualized growth and FEDFUNDS > its 12-month average
failure_condition: Reject if the CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS interaction has a nonnegative coefficient for 20-day returns in walk-forward tests or triggered returns exceed unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: A negative quarter-over-quarter change in GDPC1 signals broad real demand contraction, lowering expected earnings growth and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if negative GDPC1 growth triggers are followed by positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of observations or if the signal loses significance after real-time data revisions.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth is strong while UNRATE is low or falling, labor income supports consumption without confirming recession risk, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 1-month change > 150 thousand and UNRATE is flat or down over 1 month
failure_condition: Reject if the joint strong-labor trigger has nonpositive average 5-day forward returns or if PAYEMS strength combined with falling UNRATE underperforms unconditional 5-day returns.
